resources.string: Institute of Medical Philosophy Future AI (IMPF-AI)

This app is a study and research aid for classical Chinese medicine texts. It does not provide diagnosis or treatment advice; pattern identification and prescriptions must be determined in person by a licensed practitioner.
Server unreachable (using local corpus) · Tap to configure
e.g. What is the pathogenesis of Taiyang wind-strike?
Current: %1$s · Role ceiling is adjudicated by the server token; escalation requests are refused
Server pipeline: retrieve evidence → verify citations → reflect and repair → bind claims (the real trace is shown when done)
(The agent requested clarification — please provide fuller four-examination findings)
Not in the local corpus; tap to load from the server
I am… (affects the evidence surface and safety policy)
The role is only a request: the real ceiling is bound to the server token and cannot be escalated; patients never receive prescriptions or dosage content.
Plaintext HTTP address: development only (release builds block cleartext traffic). Use HTTPS in production.
The token is encrypted at rest via Android Keystore (EncryptedSharedPreferences).
Warning: Keystore is unavailable on this device and the token is stored in plaintext — do not store high-privilege tokens here.
This app never stores model-provider keys (OpenAI/Anthropic etc. live only on the server); this token is just a role-bound access key issued by the Hermes server.
Source texts are traditional; conversion is display-only
Bundled corpus only: clause search/reading and the formula library work; matching, the agent and commentaries need the server

%1$d entries. Records the evidence trail of every agent Q&A and formula match (question, role, backend, evidence clauses, verification result). Stored on this device only; the server keeps the authoritative audit.

Architecture: native Android client (trusted interaction + offline knowledge) + Hermes Python platform (authoritative reasoning, governance and evidence). All clinical-assist reasoning, role adjudication, patient projection and citation verification run on the server.
